edges.description: Reduced eIF2B GEF activity lowers the pool of active eIF2-GTP and causes constitutive, deregulated activation of the integrated stress response downstream of eIF2B.
Constitutive ISR activation impairs maturation and function of white matter astrocytes, the cell type selectively affected in VWM.
edges.target: Astrocyte Dysfunction and Selective Glial Vulnerability

edges.description: Astrocyte dysfunction and the resulting failure of oligodendrocyte maturation lead to progressive loss and cystic degeneration of central nervous system white matter.
edges.source: Astrocyte Dysfunction and Selective Glial Vulnerability
edges.target: Progressive White Matter Rarefaction and Cavitation

edges.description: The vulnerable, rarefying white matter is prone to acute episodic decline when challenged by physiological stress, superimposed on the chronic progressive course.
edges.source: Progressive White Matter Rarefaction and Cavitation

nodes.description: White matter astrocytes are the primary affected cell type in VWM. Impaired astrocyte maturation precedes disease onset and parallels severity; immature, morphologically abnormal astrocytes secrete factors that inhibit oligodendrocyte maturation, while wild-type astrocytes permit normal maturation of mutant oligodendrocytes. Specialized astrocytes such as cerebellar Bergmann glia and retinal Mueller cells are also involved.
nodes.id: Astrocyte Dysfunction and Selective Glial Vulnerability

evidence.reference_title: Astrocytes are central in the pathomechanisms of vanishing white matter.
evidence.snippet: In all mutant strains, impaired maturation of white matter astrocytes preceded onset and paralleled disease severity and progression.

evidence.reference_title: Astrocytes are central in the pathomechanisms of vanishing white matter.
evidence.snippet: In coculture, VWM astrocytes secreted factors that inhibited oligodendrocyte maturation, whereas WT astrocytes allowed normal maturation of VWM oligodendrocytes.

evidence.reference_title: Cortical Pathology in Vanishing White Matter.
evidence.snippet: that VWM cortical astrocytes exhibit morphological changes and are less complex in structure than control cells. Our findings also suggest that these astrocytes are immature and not reactive.

nodes.description: eIF2B is the central regulatory factor of the integrated stress response (ISR): phosphorylation of eIF2-alpha inhibits eIF2B, reducing global translation while up-regulating ISR effector mRNAs. eIF2B-deficient mutations chronically mimic this state, producing constitutive ISR activation that is specifically pronounced in astrocytes; ISR mRNA expression levels correlate with disease severity.

evidence.snippet: eIF2B is a central regulatory factor in the integrated stress response (ISR). Genetic variants decrease eIF2B activity and thereby cause constitutive activation of the ISR downstream of eIF2B. Strikingly, the ISR is specifically activated in astrocytes.

evidence.reference_title: Vanishing white matter: deregulated integrated stress response as therapy target.
evidence.snippet: In brains of VWM compared to wild-type mice we observed the most prominent changes in translation concerning ISR mRNAs; their expression levels correlated with disease severity.

nodes.description: Diffuse cerebral white matter progressively acquires a CSF-like signal on MRI and undergoes cystic degeneration (cavitation), so that increasing areas of white matter are replaced by fluid while the cortex is relatively preserved. Long-surviving patients also show secondary cortical thinning.
nodes.id: Progressive White Matter Rarefaction and Cavitation

evidence.reference_title: A new leukoencephalopathy with vanishing white matter.
evidence.snippet: In eight patients with advanced disease, MRI revealed a diffuse cerebral hemispheric leukoencephalopathy, in which increasing areas of the abnormal white matter had a signal intensity close to that of CSF on all pulse sequences.

evidence.reference_title: A new leukoencephalopathy with vanishing white matter.
evidence.snippet: Autopsy in one patient confirmed the presence of extensive cystic degeneration of the cerebral white matter with reactive change and a preserved cortex.

nodes.description: Because eIF2B governs translation during stress, the disease is dominated by chronic progressive decline punctuated by episodes of rapid, major neurological deterioration provoked by febrile infections, minor head trauma, major surgery, or acute and extreme fright, which may precipitate unexplained coma.

evidence.reference_title: Subunits of the translation initiation factor eIF2B are mutant in leukoencephalopathy with vanishing white matter.
evidence.snippet: As eIF2B has an essential role in the regulation of translation under different conditions, including stress, this may explain the rapid deterioration of people with VWM under stress.

evidence.reference_title: A new leukoencephalopathy with vanishing white matter.
evidence.snippet: There were episodes of deterioration following infections and minor head traumas, and these could results in unexplained coma.

nodes.description: Biallelic pathogenic variants in EIF2B1-EIF2B5 partially reduce the guanine nucleotide exchange activity of eIF2B, the five-subunit GEF that recycles inactive eIF2-GDP to active eIF2-GTP to permit translation initiation. eIF2B has an essential housekeeping role in translation and in its regulation during stress, yet the white matter (and ovaries) are selectively vulnerable.

evidence.reference_title: Subunits of the translation initiation factor eIF2B are mutant in leukoencephalopathy with vanishing white matter.
evidence.snippet: We have identified mutations in EIF2B5 and EIF2B2, encoding the epsilon- and beta-subunits of the translation initiation factor eIF2B and located on chromosomes 3q27 and 14q24, respectively, as causing VWM.
"Vanishing white matter" (VWM) is a leukodystrophy caused by autosomal recessive pathogenic variants in the genes encoding the subunits of eukaryotic initiation factor 2B (eIF2B).
